title: Macros 2
description: Macros can take arguments using designators like `$name:expr` for expressions.
instructions: Fix the macro so it correctly uses arguments.
hints: In Rust, macros must be defined before they are used in the source file.
Move the macro definition before the main function.
Alternatively, you can use #[macro_use] for modules.
explanation: Unlike functions, macros must be defined before they are used in the source file. This is because macro expansion happens during compilation, before the code is fully analyzed. The `$val:expr` pattern matches any Rust expression and binds it to `$val`, which can then be used in the macro body.
